_note: New York/NJ HQ data — MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford NJ. 5 group stage matches.

venue.name: MetLife Stadium
venue.address: One MetLife Stadium Dr, East Rutherford, NJ 07073
venue.notes: Largest venue in the tournament at 82,500 capacity. Clear bag policy, no re-entry. Gates open 2.5 hours before kickoff.
venue.parkingNote: Extensive on-site parking starting at $60. Reserve via FIFA app or StubHub. NJ Transit is strongly preferred — parking traffic post-match can add 60+ minutes.

transit.recommended: NJ Transit — Meadowlands Rail Line from Penn Station (direct, ~15 min ride, runs only on event days)
lines: NJ Transit Bus 320 from Port Authority
tips: The Meadowlands Rail Line runs direct from Penn Station on event days — easiest option from Manhattan.
Buy your return ticket before the match — queues at station are long post-game.
PATH train to Hoboken + Shuttle is a good backup option. Ask at Hoboken Terminal.
From Brooklyn/Queens: LIRR to Penn then Meadowlands Rail. Budget 75 min total.
transit.parking: On-site lots open 5+ hours before kickoff. $60–$90 via official app. Blue 1 lot closest to main entrance.
transit.rideshare: Designated Uber/Lyft pickup on the east service road, Gate C area. Heavy surge pricing expected — consider NJ Transit back and rideshare from Penn Station.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Flushing Meadows Corona Park
fanZones.notes: Official FIFA fan zone. Giant screens, entertainment, food from around the world. Accessible via 7 train.

fanZones.name: Hudson Yards Fan Hub
fanZones.notes: Unofficial but massive gathering point near Penn Station before Meadowlands Rail. Multiple bars and screens.

preGame.name: Hoboken bar crawl
preGame.distance: PATH from Manhattan; NJ Transit bus to stadium
preGame.notes: Hoboken's Washington St corridor is electric on match days. Marathon Bar, 1 Republik, and Green Rock fill up fast.

preGame.name: Hell's Kitchen soccer bars
preGame.distance: 10 min walk to Penn Station, then Meadowlands Rail
preGame.notes: Legends NYC, Smithfield, and Nevada Smiths (E Village) are NYC's top soccer bars. Arrive 2+ hours early.

preGame.name: Flushing Meadows Fan Festival
preGame.notes: Best pre-game energy if you want the official FIFA experience. Large, family-friendly, international food.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Brazil vs Morocco — opening weekend at the biggest venue. NYC's Brazilian community is massive and vocal. Expect 80,000+ in gold and green.
France vs Senegal — two of the tournament's most watched nations. NYC's Senegalese diaspora is the largest in the US. Les Bleus traveling support adds prestige.
Norway vs Senegal — Group I Matchday 2 with points on the line. Strong Senegalese fan presence again at MetLife. Norwegian support adds Scandinavian energy.
Ecuador vs Germany — Group E finale. NYC's large Ecuadorian community + German fans traveling in force. High stakes, colorful crowd mix.
Panama vs England — Group L finale. England fans travel in enormous numbers. NYC English ex-pat community plus Three Lions supporters from across the East Coast.

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Brazil vs Morocco opening weekend — maximum crowds. NJ Transit will be packed. Book return rail ticket in advance.
tips: Take Meadowlands Rail — driving is chaos on opening weekend
Buy round-trip rail ticket before leaving Manhattan
Arrive at Penn Station 45 min before train

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: France vs Senegal — two high-profile nations, big crowds expected. Afternoon kickoff adds post-match rush hour overlap.
tips: Meadowlands Rail recommended — post-match return before rush hour if you leave promptly
NYC's Senegalese community travels in organized groups — expect colorful energy from 11am
Hudson Yards fan bars will be packed from noon — arrive early

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Norway vs Senegal — evening match, more manageable than opening weekend. NJ Transit runs efficiently.
tips: Evening kickoff — avoid NJ Transit during 5pm rush hour by dining near Penn first
Post-match transit back to Penn straightforward
One of the more relaxed MetLife match days logistically

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Ecuador vs Germany — Group E finale, high stakes. German fans travel in massive numbers; NYC Ecuadorian community adds to crowd.
tips: German fans fill every bar from the Flushing fan zone to Hoboken by noon
Meadowlands Rail required — parking will be full by 11am
Walk to secondary pickup zone for post-match rideshare if rail is too crowded

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Panama vs England — England fans are the world's largest traveling fan base. NYC English ex-pat bars packed all day.
tips: English fans travel in enormous numbers — book pub seat near Penn well in advance
Meadowlands Rail is the only realistic option for this match
Group stage final weekend — MetLife atmosphere at its peak

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil vs Morocco at the world's biggest World Cup venue. 82,500 fans, opening weekend, NYC energy at full blast. This is a once-in-a-generation event in your city.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: France vs Senegal at MetLife — two of the tournament's most watched nations and NYC's Senegalese diaspora is the largest in the US. This match hits different in New York.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Norway vs Senegal with Group I points on the line. Strong Senegalese fan presence continues at MetLife; Norwegian support adds Scandinavian flavor. Good evening atmosphere.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Ecuador vs Germany — Group E finale at MetLife. Germany fans are the most organized traveling support in the world. NYC's Ecuadorian community makes this a genuine local derby atmosphere.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Panama vs England — England's traveling support is legendary and NYC's English ex-pat bars will be electric all day. Group L finale with real stakes for both teams.
